refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of what 'availability' means (uptime ratio), the measurement period, and inclusion of safety-driven constraints. High safety criticality means unavailability due to safety interventions must be distinguished from failure-driven unavailability in root cause analysis.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of system state transitions (operational, maintenance, safety-hold, failed) with timestamped records; post-evaluation statistical analysis of uptime ratio against the 0.97 threshold; traceability to failure modes and safety events.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all state transitions (operational, maintenance, safety-hold, failure) with timestamp and root cause.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall minimize unplanned downtime through predictive maintenance and fault tolerance mechanisms.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance teams.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall support root cause analysis and corrective action tracking for all downtime events.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and reporting system that aggregates telemetry from all subsystems, logs state transitions, calculates rolling availability metrics, and triggers alerts when availability falls below target.

DP-B: Distribute availability monitoring across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with local state tracking and periodic synchronization to a central dashboard; rely on manual log review for root cause analysis.
DP-C: Implement redundant Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems with automatic failover to maintain continuous operation; couple with a lightweight availability tracker that records failover events.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and reporting system that aggregates telemetry from all subsystems, logs state transitions, calculates rolling availability metrics, and triggers alerts when availability falls below target.

potential_sources: Coupling to Vehicle subsystem via Telemetry interface (input dependency on vehicle state signals).
Coupling to Teleoperation subsystem via Telemetry interface (input dependency on tele-operator availability and command latency).
Coupling to Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface (input dependency on safety event notifications).
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits input coupling (receives data from multiple subsystems) but maintains logical independence in availability calculation logic. Coupling is acceptable because it is unidirectional and well-defined via standard interfaces. DP-A does not dictate how Vehicle or Teleoperation operate; it only observes and reports. This is a classic observer pattern and does not violate independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: All subsystems reliably emit telemetry and safety alerts via defined interfaces; risk: if telemetry is lost, availability calculation becomes unreliable.
Assumption: Availability target of 0.97 is achievable with current Vehicle and Teleoperation designs; risk: if subsystems have inherent failure rates that prevent 0.97, the DP cannot compensate.
Assumption: Root cause analysis can be performed post-hoc from logged state transitions; risk: if state transitions are not granular enough, root cause remains ambiguous.

Risk: High safety criticality means safety-driven shutdowns must be clearly distinguished from failures in availability reporting; misclassification could mask safety issues or inflate apparent availability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly define: (1) the set of telemetry signals required from each subsystem, (2) the state machine for availability (operational, maintenance, safety-hold, failed), (3) the calculation formula for rolling availability (e.g., uptime in last 30 days / total scheduled time), and (4) the alert threshold and escalation path when availability drops below 0.97.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: The Safety Services module is assigned ownership of availability monitoring, state transition logging, availability metric calculation, and alerting. This module will ingest Telemetry and SafetyAlert inputs from Vehicle and Teleoperation, maintain a centralized availability database, compute rolling availability metrics, and emit availability status updates and alerts.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Input from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; carries vehicle state (operational, maintenance, failed), tele-operator status, command latency, and system health indicators.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Input from Safety Services internal safety monitoring; carries safety event notifications, safety-hold triggers, and safety-driven shutdown events.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Output to Site Manager and Maintenance teams; carries availability status, rolling availability metric, alerts when availability falls below target, and root cause summaries.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. Availability monitoring is a system-level concern that logically belongs at the top level. However, assigning to NGHE Solution would create a diffuse responsibility (NGHE Solution would also own Vehicle and Teleoperation), making ownership unclear. Containment is moderate because availability is a system KPI, not a subsystem function.

Vehicle is a subsystem focused on mechanical and electrical operation. Availability monitoring is a cross-system concern that should not be owned by a single subsystem. Assigning to Vehicle would create high diffusion (Vehicle would need to know about Teleoperation and Safety Services) and poor separation of concerns.

Teleoperation is a subsystem focused on remote operation. Availability monitoring is a cross-system concern. Assigning to Teleoperation would create high diffusion and poor separation of concerns, similar to Vehicle.

Safety Services is the natural owner of availability monitoring because: (1) it already ingests safety-critical telemetry and alerts, (2) it has the authority to declare safety-driven shutdowns, (3) availability is a safety and operational KPI that must be tracked alongside safety events, (4) it can distinguish safety-driven unavailability from failure-driven unavailability, (5) it has low diffusion because it does not need to own Vehicle or Teleoperation logic. Containment is high.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the optimal owner because availability monitoring is a cross-system observational function that naturally aligns with safety oversight. Safety Services already aggregates safety-critical data and has the authority to declare system states (safe, unsafe, safety-hold). Availability monitoring is a natural extension of this responsibility. The module has low diffusion because it does not need to own or modify Vehicle or Teleoperation logic; it only observes and reports.
containment_risks: Risk: If Safety Services is overloaded with too many responsibilities (safety monitoring, availability tracking, alerting, root cause analysis), it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure. Mitigation: Clearly separate safety monitoring (safety-critical) from availability reporting (operational) within Safety Services, with independent data paths and alert channels.

Risk: Availability monitoring may be perceived as a non-safety function and deprioritized within Safety Services. Mitigation: Establish clear SLAs for availability metric calculation and alerting; treat availability as a safety-relevant KPI because downtime can impact safety planning.

Risk: If Safety Services is not yet fully designed, assigning availability monitoring may create scope creep. Mitigation: Defer detailed design of Safety Services until all cross-system observational functions are identified and consolidated.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Safety Services has the capacity and authority to own availability monitoring. If Safety Services is already overloaded, consider creating a dedicated 'System Observability' or 'Operations Monitoring' module that sits at the NGHE Solution level and owns availability, performance, and operational metrics. For now, Safety Services is the best fit given the available modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized availability monitoring) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP provides a mechanism to measure, track, and alert on availability metrics. The DP is logically sound and does not introduce unnecessary complexity. The coupling to subsystems is acceptable because it is unidirectional and well-defined.

diffusion_risks: Safety Services must ingest Telemetry from both Vehicle and Teleoperation; if either subsystem fails to emit telemetry, availability calculation becomes unreliable. Mitigation: Define telemetry SLAs and implement fallback logic (e.g., assume unavailable if telemetry is not received within a timeout).

Safety Services must distinguish safety-driven shutdowns from failures; if SafetyAlert interface does not clearly indicate the reason for shutdown, root cause analysis will be ambiguous. Mitigation: Extend SafetyAlert to include shutdown reason codes (e.g., 'safety-hold', 'maintenance', 'failure').
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services is the optimal container for availability monitoring. The module has clear ownership boundaries and low diffusion. External interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) are well-defined and align with existing system architecture.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via continuous telemetry logging and post-evaluation statistical analysis. The 0.97 availability target is measurable and can be validated against logged state transitions. Root cause analysis is traceable to specific downtime events.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Safety Services owns availability monitoring, state transition logging, metric calculation, and alerting. Vehicle and Teleoperation own the subsystem reliability and telemetry emission. Site Manager and Maintenance own the operational policies and corrective actions.

recommended_revisions: Refine the Telemetry interface to explicitly include vehicle state (operational, maintenance, failed), tele-operator availability, and command latency. Define telemetry emission frequency and SLAs.
Extend the SafetyAlert interface to include shutdown reason codes (safety-hold, maintenance, failure) to enable accurate root cause classification.
Define the availability calculation formula explicitly (e.g., uptime in last 30 days / total scheduled time) and the rolling window for metric updates (e.g., daily, weekly).
Establish alert thresholds and escalation paths when availability falls below 0.97 (e.g., notify Site Manager, trigger maintenance review).
Define the root cause analysis process and the data retention policy for availability logs (e.g., retain for 2 years for audit and trend analysis).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-functions (state transition detection, metric calculation, alerting, root cause analysis). The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs that are not yet atomic and require further decomposition. DP-A is sound and well-aligned with Safety Services, but the implementation details (telemetry signals, state machine, calculation formula, alert logic) must be refined at the next level. Proceeding to decompose sub-FRs will clarify the detailed design and identify any additional coupling or diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all state transitions (operational, maintenance, safety-hold, failure) with timestamp and root cause.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall minimize unplanned downtime through predictive maintenance and fault tolerance mechanisms.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time availability status visibility to Site Manager and Maintenance teams.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall support root cause analysis and corrective action tracking for all downtime events.

assumptions: All subsystems reliably emit telemetry and safety alerts via defined interfaces.
Availability target of 0.97 is achievable with current Vehicle and Teleoperation designs.
Root cause analysis can be performed post-hoc from logged state transitions.
Safety Services has the capacity and authority to own availability monitoring.
Site Manager and Maintenance teams have the operational policies and processes to act on availability alerts.
open_risks: If telemetry is lost or delayed, availability calculation becomes unreliable; mitigation: define telemetry SLAs and implement fallback logic.
If SafetyAlert does not clearly indicate shutdown reason, root cause analysis will be ambiguous; mitigation: extend SafetyAlert with reason codes.
If Safety Services is overloaded, availability monitoring may be deprioritized; mitigation: establish clear SLAs and consider dedicated observability module.
If availability target of 0.97 is not achievable with current subsystem designs, the DP cannot compensate; mitigation: conduct feasibility study and adjust target if needed.
evidence_needed: Telemetry interface specification (signals, frequency, SLAs) from Vehicle and Teleoperation.
SafetyAlert interface specification (event types, reason codes) from Safety Services.
Availability calculation formula and rolling window definition from System Engineer.
Alert threshold and escalation path definition from Site Manager and Safety Manager.
Root cause analysis process and data retention policy from Maintenance/Quality.
Feasibility study on achieving 0.97 availability with current subsystem designs.
